techcrunch.title: Notion just turned its workspace into a hub for AI agents
techcrunch.summary: Notion’s new developer platform lets teams connect AI agents, external data sources, and custom code directly into their workspace as the company pushes deeper into agentic productivity software.

techcrunch.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
techcrunch.summary: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

techcrunch.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
techcrunch.summary: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

techcrunch.title: Who trusts Sam Altman?
techcrunch.summary: "I believe I am an honest and trustworthy businessperson," Altman testified in federal court.

techcrunch.title: Origin Lab raises $8M to help video game companies sell data to world-model builders
techcrunch.summary: Origin Lab will serve as a marketplace where AI labs can buy high-quality licensed data, and video-game companies can sell it.

techcrunch.title: Anthropic courts a new kind of customer: small business owners
techcrunch.summary: For founders and investors, Anthropic's new offering signals that the AI platform wars are expanding downmarket and that the next major battleground for user acquisition isn't the Fortune 500; it's the 36 million small businesses that make up the backbone of the U.S. economy.

techcrunch.title: Amazon launches an AI shopping assistant for the search bar, powered by Alexa+
techcrunch.summary: Alexa for Shopping offers a voice- and touch-enabled shopping experience across mobile, desktop, and Echo Show smart displays. Alexa for Shopping provides more personalized recommendations and automates the shopping experience across Amazon and other online retailers.

techcrunch.title: Anthropic now has more business customers than OpenAI, according to Ramp data
techcrunch.summary: A survey compiled from fintech firm Ramp’s clients’ expense data shows 34.4% of participating businesses are paying for Anthropic services, more than any other AI lab, while only 32.3% pay for OpenAI.

techcrunch.title: Introducing the 6 stages at TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 — built for today’s tougher startup market
techcrunch.summary: From October 13-15, TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 will feature 200+ sessions across six stages, led by 250+ tech leaders shaping the industry today. Register now to save up to $410, plus 50% off a second pass.

techcrunch.title: WhatsApp adds an incognito mode in Meta AI chats
techcrunch.summary: Meta said these incognito conversations are not saved, and messages will disappear by default once you close the chat.
